title: Significance of the actual moon
wikitext:
[]: Significance of the actual moon?             Quote Post by Erizo » Fri Aug 19, 2011 8:29 am Now this may be a noob question but I did sought around and couldn't find an answer to it. So White Moon is in Antartica, Black Moon is somewhere beneath Japan. What exactly IS the moon that they see in the sky then? Just a normal, good ol' moon?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Aug 19, 2011 8:55 am Yeah, basically. Big chunk of rock created when the Black Moon first slammed into the Earth however many billion years ago. In a way, you could argue that Lilith was responsible for its creation, which is why Rei is sometimes associated with the moon.

[]:             Quote Post by Erizo » Fri Aug 19, 2011 9:13 am Thank you. And I was thinking there is this third angel who just happened to be nice enough not to disturb Adam and Lilith engaging in some pre-ancestral hentai action.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Fri Aug 19, 2011 10:17 am There's also a lot of yin-yang imagery going on with the moon. Lilim - associated with moon. Angels - associated with sun.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Aug 19, 2011 12:36 pm That might be a bit more of a stretch. The sun is more generally associated with choice, adversity, challenge than Angels specifically - it's also used to represent Asuka a few times, remember.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Aug 19, 2011 2:51 pm I agree with Azathoth. Any Yin/Yang imagery occurring is between Asuka and Rei, not Adam and Lilith.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Fri Aug 19, 2011 2:52 pm Trajan wrote: Lilim - associated with moon. Angels - associated with sun.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Fri Aug 19, 2011 3:03 pm Surely something can be read from the fact that all the Angels appear during the daytime?

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Fri Aug 19, 2011 3:25 pm Rei = moon Asuka = sun I picked that one up from the wiki. In typical Evangelion fashion, make of it what you will.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Aug 19, 2011 4:25 pm Trajan wrote: Surely something can be read from the fact that all the Angels appear during the daytime?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Aug 19, 2011 5:14 pm Xard wrote: Sachiel and Ramiel showdowns took place during night

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Aug 19, 2011 6:10 pm Azathoth wrote: Both arrive/initially attack during the day though.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Aug 19, 2011 6:18 pm "They shall all be giant monsters of fearsome might that look like they were pulled from the ass of Satan, except for the last one who's a nubile pretty boy, yet the dark scares the shit out of them so don't bother keeping personnel on duty" - the Dead Sea Scrolls Alternative answer: Rei probably doesn't sleep much.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Aug 19, 2011 6:23 pm Azathoth wrote: Alternative answer: Rei probably doesn't sleep much.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Aug 19, 2011 6:38 pm Well, maybe at night Gendou puts on HIS plugsuit and gets ready for trouble cue that image

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Sat Aug 20, 2011 11:51 pm Azathoth wrote: Well, maybe at night Gendou puts on HIS plugsuit and gets ready for trouble cue that image
summary:
[]: Significance of the actual moon?             Quote Post by Erizo » Fri Aug 19, 2011 8:29 am Now this may be a noob question but I did sought around and couldn't find an answer to it. So White Moon is in Antartica, Black Moon is somewhere beneath Japan. What exactly IS the moon that they see in the sky then? Just a normal, good ol' moon?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth &ra
